decisions.title: Formalize Sift Edge Production Readiness Criteria
decisions.context: Multiple Edge signals (metrics, syncing prototype, query issues) suggest we're advancing Edge capabilities but lack clear production gates. With Edge being a 'Build the Moat' pillar, we need explicit readiness criteria before broader customer rollout.

arguments.for: Prevents premature customer deployments that could damage trust; ensures Edge meets quality bar for 'Embed Deeper' strategy; creates clear accountability for launch decisions.
arguments.against: May slow down early customer adoption that could provide valuable feedback; formal criteria can become bureaucratic gates; some issues only surface in production.

decisions.title: Prioritize E1 to E2 Migration Timeline vs New Feature Development
decisions.context: E1→E2 migration work (ENG-8991) competes with new Canvas polish, Reports polish, and relative time compare features. Explore V2 (E2) is critical to 'Nail the Core', but customer-facing polish drives immediate decision capture.

arguments.for: E2 migration unlocks future velocity and is foundational to core experience; delaying creates technical debt and customer confusion; aligns with 'Nail the Core' pillar focus.
arguments.against: Customers aren't asking for migration, they want polish and new features; migration doesn't directly increase North Star metric; can phase migration over longer timeline.

decisions.title: Establish Data Layer V3 Observability Standards Before Rollout
decisions.context: 10+ tickets focused on DLv3 monitoring (alerts for disk usage, latency, error rates across data-batcher, catalog, pts-id-service). New Slack channel created (ENG-8986). This suggests DLv3 is approaching production but observability is immature.
decisions.blockedBy: Need to establish baseline thresholds from current production data; unclear who owns on-call rotation for DLv3 alerts

arguments.for: DLv3 is critical infrastructure for 'Embed Deeper' at scale; proper monitoring prevents customer-facing incidents; establishes operational maturity culture.
arguments.against: Can implement alerts reactively based on incidents; over-monitoring creates alert fatigue; teams can monitor their own services without standards.

decisions.title: Approve Authentication Caching Architecture (ENG-8821)
decisions.context: LRU caching for auth impacts security posture, latency, and system architecture. High CPU usage from Amplitude client (ENG-8158) suggests performance optimization is needed, but auth caching has security implications.

arguments.for: Reduces latency for every API call, improving 'Nail the Core' experience; addresses performance issues systematically; standard pattern in industry.
arguments.against: Cache invalidation complexity for permission changes; potential security risk if cache not properly bounded; adds complexity to authentication flow.

decisions.title: Define Python Rules Launch Scope and Timeline
decisions.context: Migration of sift_py modules (ENG-8910) suggests Python Rules infrastructure maturing. Python Rules is a key 2026 product for 'Build the Moat', but no clear launch signals in current work.

arguments.for: Python Rules differentiates from competitors and increases switching costs; needed for complex decision logic customers request; aligns with moat-building strategy.
arguments.against: Adds maintenance burden of Python runtime; customers may prefer no-code solutions; could delay polish on existing features.

alerts: Back-to-back meetings 1pm-5pm limit deep work on critical decisions
3 one-on-ones today - consider batching to preserve context switching
Meeting load 80% - exceeds healthy threshold of 60% for senior IC/leadership
Only 8 focus hours across entire week for strategic thinking
No decision-making sessions scheduled despite 5 open strategic decisions

timeAudit.weekSummary: Critical imbalance: 32 meeting hours vs 8 focus hours. With 5 strategic decisions pending (Edge readiness, E2 migration priority, DLv3 observability, auth caching, Python Rules scope), you need dedicated decision-making time. Recommend: block Fri morning for decision synthesis, delegate 2-3 recurring meetings, convert 2 status meetings to async updates.

events.title: Auth Caching Architecture Decision Due
events.description: Security + Engineering must approve LRU caching implementation for authentication. Impacts API performance across platform.

events.title: E2 Migration vs Feature Development Prioritization
events.description: Leadership decision on resource allocation between E1→E2 migration and customer-facing polish work. Affects Q1 delivery commitments.

events.title: DLv3 Observability Standards Deadline
events.description: Complete monitoring standards for data-batcher, catalog, pts-id-service before broader DLv3 rollout. Currently blocked on baseline thresholds.

events.title: Sift Edge Production Readiness Criteria
events.description: Define formal launch criteria for Edge including metrics, syncing, query stability. Gates customer deployments.

events.title: Python Rules Scope & Timeline Planning
events.description: Define launch scope for Python Rules including beta customer criteria, feature set, and H1 timeline. Key moat-building initiative.

events.title: DLv3 Alert System Operational Review
events.description: Review all DLv3 monitoring alerts (disk usage, latency, error rates) after first week of production monitoring.

events.title: Q1 Strategy Checkpoint: Pillar Health
events.description: Review progress on Nail the Core (at_risk), Embed Deeper (on_track), Build the Moat (at_risk). Adjust H1 priorities based on pillar health.

events.title: North Star Metric Review: Decision Capture
events.description: Monthly review of Decisions Captured Per Customer (currently 147, +12%). Analyze drivers and lagging segments.

events.title: E2 Migration Completion Target
events.description: Target completion for E1→E2 customer migrations. Enables deprecation of E1 infrastructure and unlocks E2-only features.

events.title: Sift Edge Beta Expansion Review
events.description: Assess Edge beta performance (metrics, syncing stability) to approve expansion from 8 to 25 deployments.

events.title: Python Rules Private Beta Launch
events.description: Launch Python Rules to first 5 beta customers. Key differentiation play for Build the Moat pillar.
